primary_concern: Sepsis with concurrent acute kidney injury and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Patient demonstrates sepsis-3 criteria with elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L), leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL). Platelets are persistently low (<100 K/uL) with progressive AKI (Stage 3 documented). Bilirubin elevation suggests possible hepatic involvement. No clinical notes provided to confirm infection source.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (Stage 3) with prior 2.1 mg/dL (Stage 1) confirms progressive AKI
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score 3; <50 K/uL indicates severe coagulopathy
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local protocol
Perform focused infection source control evaluation
Initiate fluid resuscitation and reassess perfusion
Monitor platelet trends and consider transfusion if <20 K/uL
Order renal replacement therapy consultation for Stage 3 AKI
doctor_handoff: Review for hypotension/vasopressor requirements to confirm septic shock. Evaluate for coagulopathy progression (INR, fibrinogen). Confirm infection source with imaging/labs.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
